nameMeanings.name: Yorke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: York
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of York in northern England. The surname is now widespread throughout England. Originally, the city bore the Latin name Eburacum, which is probably from a Brittonic name meaning ‘yew-tree place’. This was altered by folk etymology to Old English Eoforwīc (from the elements eofor ‘wild boar’ + wīc ‘specialized farmstead’). This name was taken over by Scandinavian settlers, who altered it back to opacity in the form Jórvík or Jórk (English York, which became finally settled as the placename in the 13th century). The surname has also been adopted by Jews as an Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of the East Slavic patronymic   Yurkovich   or its Croatian, Slovak, or Slovenian variants. Compare   Yurk  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Yorke was born in 1470, in Melksham, Wiltshire, England, his father, Ralph St Leger, was 40 and his mother, Anne Prophett, was 38. He had at least 2 sons with Margaret Self. He died in 1528, in Naseby, Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Yorke est né en 1470, à Melksham, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Ralph St Leger, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Anne Prophett, avait 38 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Margaret Self. Il est décédé en 1528, à Naseby, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Yorke 1470 in Melksham, Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph St Leger 40 und seine Mutter Anne Prophett 38. Er hatte mit Margaret Self mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1528 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Naseby, Northamptonshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Yorke è nato il nel 1470 nel Melksham, Wiltshire, England, il suo padre, Ralph St Leger, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Anne Prophett, aveva 38 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Margaret Self. Morì nel 1528, nel Naseby, Northamptonshire, England, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Yorkeが1470年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアメルクシャムで生まれた時，父Ralph St Legerは40歳，母Anne Prophettは38歳であった。彼はMargaret Selfとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1528年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアナセビーで58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Yorke(이)가 1470년 영국 윌츠히르 멜크샤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph St Leger(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Prophett(은)는 38세였다. 그는 Margaret Self(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1528년 영국 노르섬프토실르 나세비에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Yorke nasceu em 1470, em Melksham, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ralph St Leger, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Anne Prophett, tinha 38 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Margaret Self. Ele faleceu em 1528, em Naseby, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Yorke родился в 1470 г. в местности мелкшам, уилцхир, Англия, его отцу по имени Ralph St Leger было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Prophett, 38. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Margaret Self. Он умер в 1528 г. в местности неисеби, нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 58 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Yorke nació en 1470, en Melksham, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Ralph St Leger, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Anne Prophett, tenía 38 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Margaret Self. Murió en 1528, en Naseby, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 58 años.
